## 🏷️ Type of Change

<!-- Mark all that apply with [x]. -->

- [ ] 🐛 **Bug fix** (non-breaking change which fixes an issue)
- [ ] ✨ **New feature** (non-breaking change which adds functionality)
- [ ] 🎨 **UI / UX polish** (visual tweaks, new animations, theming)
- [ ] ♻️ **Refactor** (code restructuring without behavior change)
- [ ] ⚡ **Performance** improvement
- [ ] ♿ **Accessibility** improvement
- [ ] 📝 **Documentation** only
- [ ] 🧪 **Tests** added or updated
- [ ] 🔧 **Build / CI / tooling** change
- [ ] 💥 **Breaking change** (would cause existing functionality to not work as expected)

---

## 🧪 How Has This Been Tested?

<!-- Describe the testing you performed. Builds pass? Manual click-through of affected pages? Unit tests added? -->

- [ ] `dotnet build -c Debug -p:Platform=$Platform` completes with no new warnings
- [ ] Manually tested on: <!-- e.g. Windows 11 24H2 / x64 -->
- [ ] Unit tests added / updated (if applicable)
- [ ] Screen-reader / keyboard navigation verified (for UI changes)

## ✅ Checklist

<!-- Confirm every applicable item before requesting review. -->

- [ ] My code follows the style and patterns described in [`CONTRIBUTING.md`](../CONTRIBUTING.md)
- [ ] I have performed a self-review of my own code
- [ ] I have commented hard-to-understand areas of code
- [ ] I have added / updated XML doc comments on new public APIs
- [ ] I have updated the documentation ([`README.md`](../README.md), [`ARCHITECTURE.md`](../ARCHITECTURE.md)) where needed
- [ ] I have added an entry to [`CHANGELOG.md`](../CHANGELOG.md) under `[Unreleased]` (for user-visible changes)
- [ ] No new warnings are introduced by my changes
- [ ] My commit messages follow the [Conventional Commits](https://www.conventionalcommits.org/) convention
- [ ] I have respected accessibility (AutomationProperties, keyboard nav, contrast) for any UI changes
